Off Calendar Oct-15-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
James Parker, Esq. of Hugo Parker, Tel. 415.264.4969, appearing for Intervenor;
No appearance by any other party or counsel.

This matter is contested. Mr. Parker states that he is not contesting the tentative ruling. Mr. Parker states that this morning's appearance is to inform the court that the judgment against Defendant Cobbledick-Kibbe Glass Company will need to be set aside. Brief discussions are held.

Discussions conclude, and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows and sets a case management conference:

Intervenor Employers Insurance of Wausau, as an Insurer of Wausau, as an Insurer of Cobbledick-Kibbe Glass Company, a Terminated California Corporation's Motion to Aside the Default and Default Judgment is GRANTED, in part, and DENIED, in part. No opposition filed.

Intervenor's Motion to Set Aside Default is DENIED. It is unnecessary to set aside Cobbledick-Kibbe, Inc's entry of default because an insured's default has no effect on the insurer. (See Western Heritage Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (2011) 199 Cal.App.4th 1196, 1207.)

Intervenor's Motion to Set Aside Default Judgment is GRANTED under C.C.P. section 473. The default judgment entered against Cobbledick-Kibbe, Inc. is set aside only as related to Intervenor.

A Case Management Conference is set for Tuesday, November 5, 2024, at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 301. Case management statements are due by Tuesday, October 29, 2024.

Order signed.

Judge: Rochelle C. East. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Not Reported. Bailiff: Not Present. (301/RCE)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order (1) Setting Aside Default And Default Judgment, And (2) For Leave To File An Answer In Intervention; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar May-14-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances, not contested, and the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Proposed Intervenor Nationwide Mutual Insurance Company's Motion for Order (1) Setting Aside Default and Default Judgement, and (2) For Leave to File an Answer In Intervention is DENIED.

Default Judgment was entered against insured Cobbledick-Kibbe, Inc. in 2016. As a general rule, the choice between whether to seek intervention or move to set the default judgment aside will be determined by the stage the proceedings have reached when the insurer seeks to become involved. (Western Heritage Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (2011) 199 Cal.App.4th 1196, 1210.) An insurer can move to intervene prior to the entry of judgment, or move to set aside the default judgment if that judgment has already been entered. (Id., citing Reliance Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (2000) 84 Cal.App.4th 383, 387.)

While Nationwide's motion is styled as a motion to set aside and a motion to intervene, the memorandum of points and authorities does not expressly argue for either vacating the default or the default judgment, nor does it provide any statutory authority or grounds for such relief. The moving papers are dedicated entirely to why Nationwide should be permitted to intervene. But before any request to intervene is granted, the default judgment must be first properly set aside. Nationwide's motion is not a motion to set aside, but rather a motion to intervene with an ancillary request to set aside the default judgment, unsupported by any authority or evidence.

The Court notes two further defects. First, this motion was set on 15-days' notice in violation of CCP Section 1005(b), which requires at least 16 court days' notice before the hearing. Nationwide also did not include the two additional court days that must be added for electronic service. (CCP Section 1010.6). Second, the POS does not contain the persons served with the instant motion, and instead states that the documents were served "on all counsel of record." Any POS filed in this case should contain the names of the persons served and their email addresses if served electronically. Defects of this nature in the future will result in the motion being taken off calendar.

The moving party is to prepare proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and submit it to Department 301 for review and signature.

Judge: Rochelle C. East. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Matter Not Reported. Court Reporter: Bailiff: Not Present. (301/RCE)

The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is June 9, 2016. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone.
